extra.reasoning: Decision system, 7 boolean inputs. Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). Only rule that sets rec_drug_a true is r5 (priority 50), conditioned on the 5-way conjunction C = (hypertension AND g6pd_deficiency AND peptic_ulcer AND diabetes AND hepatic_impairment); otherwise rec_drug_a defaults false. So rec_drug_a = true if and only if C holds. For rec_drug_b: r4 (priority 50) sets it true when hypertension holds, while r3 (priority 90) sets it false when C holds; default false otherwise. Whenever C holds (the only way to get rec_drug_a = true), both r3 and r4 match rec_drug_b, and priority resolution selects the higher priority r3 (90 > 50, no tie), forcing rec_drug_b := false. Therefore in every state where rec_drug_a = true, rec_drug_b is forced false by the higher-priority r3. Conversely, rec_drug_b = true requires r4 to win, which requires C false (so r3 does not fire), but C false means r5 does not fire and rec_drug_a defaults to false. The two outputs can never be jointly true. Concrete check at H=G=P=D=Hep=true: rec_drug_a=true (r5), rec_drug_b=false (r3 beats r4) -> property satisfied. The property holds over the entire input space.
